subtasks.description: Query TPC-H database to identify top 10 customers by lifetime value (total revenue) filtered to BUILDING market segment, returning custkey, name, and aggregate metrics (total spend, order count, recency)

For each of the top 10 customers from sql_top10_customers, reconcile their lifetime order pattern (order frequency, volume trends, product category preferences) with their segment positioning, then generate a personalized retention narrative referencing their specific order behavior and value contribution

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks, minimum necessary decomposition: (1) sql_gen (mid-tier) to aggregate TPC-H with multi-table join (customer, orders, lineitem) and market segment filter—moderate complexity warranting mid-tier. (2) cross_recon (frontier-tier) takes the 10 customer profiles and must synthesize order pattern data (frequency, recency, category affinity) with personalization logic to generate individualized retention narratives—this inference and multi-signal narrative generation requires frontier capability. The reconciliation step naturally combines customer behavior analysis with narrative generation, avoiding unnecessary subtask splitting.

output.sql: SELECT c.c_custkey, c.c_name, c.c_mktsegment, SUM(o.o_totalprice) AS total_spend, COUNT(o.o_orderkey) AS order_count, MAX(o.o_orderdate) AS last_order_date, MIN(o.o_orderdate) AS first_order_date FROM customer c JOIN orders o ON c.c_custkey = o.o_custkey WHERE c.c_mktsegment = 'BUILDING' GROUP BY c.c_custkey, c.c_name, c.c_mktsegment ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 10
